_**User**_

@clash-verge-rev 
1： "selected-timeout": 3000,

这个不需要再客户端界面配置，我手动修改配置文件就可以。 请你移除相关代码

2： Set 里这次“可选地测一次速”用的是固定 5 秒超时，和你配置的 timeout: 250 或 selected-timeout 都无关。
若节点在你这边本来就慢（例如 >5s 才通），这次测速很容易失败，节点会保持不 alive；紧接着 getProxies() 时 findAliveProxy 就会清空选择并 fallback，now 一变，通知就出现。
若你给该组配了 selected-timeout（例如 2000），findAliveProxy 会用 2 秒再测一次选定节点；但 Set 里那次仍然是 5 秒，所以“选节点瞬间”是否被判活，还是受这次 5 秒测速影响。

这里的测速逻辑是不是有一点冗余了

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The user is asking about two things related to clash-verge-rev:

1. Remove the "selected-timeout": 3000 configuration from the client UI - they want to manually edit the config file instead. So I need to find and remove the related code for selected-timeout in the client interface.

2. They're describing a redundancy in the speed test logic:
- In "Set" (when selecting a node), there's a fixed 5 second timeout for the optional speed test, which is independent of the configured timeout: 250 or selected-timeout
- If a node is slow (e.g. >5s to connect), this speed test can fail, the node stays "not alive", then when getProxies() runs, findAliveProxy clears the selection and fallback happens, "now" changes, and a notification appears
- If you configure selected-timeout (e.g. 2000) for the group, findAliveProxy will use 2 seconds to test the selected node again; but the test in "Set" is still 5 seconds, so whether the "selected node moment" is judged as alive still depends on this 5 second speed test
- They're asking if this speed test logic is somewhat redundant

I will search the clash-verge-rev codebase for:
1. selected-timeout related code to remove from UI
2. The speed test logic - the 5 second timeout in Set, findAliveProxy, etc.
</details></think>

If we remove the field from the form, we need to make sure when we load a group that has "selected-timeout", we don't lose it when saving. So we should: (1) Not show the field in UI, (2) When loading group into form, we can leave defaultValues without selected-timeout; when building/saving the group config from form values, we need to preserve "selected-timeout" from the original group if it exists (so we don't strip it). Actually re-reading the user message - they said they will manually edit the config file for selected-timeout. So when the client saves the profile (e.g. reorder groups, change other fields), if we omit selected-timeout from the form's output, we might delete it from the config. So we need to either: (a) When serializing group to yaml, preserve selected-timeout from the loaded group if it existed (pass through), or (b) Not include the key in the form at all so when we build the group object we don't set selected-timeout - but then when we dump prependSeq/appendSeq to yaml, the group objects in the list might not have selected-timeout. So the safe approach is: when we build each group for save, copy over selected-timeout from the original group (from fetchContent) if present. That way manual edits in the file are preserved when the user does other edits in the UI. I will look at how group list is used when saving.

Looking at the code flow: fetchContent loads prepend/append from yaml. So when we save we do buildGroupsYaml(prependSeq, appendSeq, deleteSeq). The prependSeq and appendSeq are the state - when user edits a group in the modal, they're editing one group and then that gets merged back.
